root_text.sn55.30:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.sn55.30:1.2: Atha kho nandako licchavimahāmatto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho nandakaṁ licchavimahāmattaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.30:2.1: “Catūhi kho, nandaka, dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako sotāpanno hoti avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇo.
root_text.sn55.30:2.3: Idha, nandaka, ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.30:2.4: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.30:2.7: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgato hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.30:2.8: Imehi kho, nandaka, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako sotāpanno hoti avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇo.
root_text.sn55.30:3.1: Imehi ca pana, nandaka, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako āyunā saṁyutto hoti dibbenapi mānusenapi;
root_text.sn55.30:3.6: Taṁ kho panāhaṁ, nandaka, nāññassa samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā sutvā vadāmi. Api ca yadeva mayā sāmaṁ ñātaṁ sāmaṁ diṭṭhaṁ sāmaṁ viditaṁ, tadevāhaṁ vadāmī”ti.
root_text.sn55.30:4.1: Evaṁ vutte, aññataro puriso nandakaṁ licchavimahāmattaṁ etadavoca:

translation_text.sn55.30:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.sn55.30:1.2: Then Nandaka the Licchavi chief minister went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.sn55.30:2.1: “Nandaka, a noble disciple who has four things is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.
translation_text.sn55.30:2.3: It’s when a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.30:2.7: And they have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.30:2.8: A noble disciple who has these four things is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.
translation_text.sn55.30:3.1: A noble disciple who has these four things is guaranteed long life, both human and heavenly …
translation_text.sn55.30:3.5: and sovereignty, both human and heavenly.
translation_text.sn55.30:3.6: Now, I don’t say this because I’ve heard it from some other ascetic or brahmin. I only say it because I’ve known, seen, and realized it for myself.”
translation_text.sn55.30:4.1: When he had spoken, a certain person said to Nandaka:
translation_text.sn55.30:4.3: “Enough now, I must say, with that exterior bath.
translation_text.sn55.30:4.4: This interior bathing will do for me, that is,
